category_title: Students with 'LD' labels who interpret figurative language using academic vocabulary
scenario_title: Whole-group discussion analyzing figurative language amidst tumultuous crosstalk
minimal_context: The class is participating in a whole-group discussion about the book 'Teammates', specifically analyzing the meaning of the figurative phrase 'Pee Wee's legs felt heavy'.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are discussing the book 'Teammates'. What does the phrase 'Pee Wee's legs felt heavy' mean?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Asserting his interpretation into the tumultuous crosstalk) He don't want to do it, and at the same time, he does.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Avoiding getting caught not knowing) I guess so.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Arranging to not look incompetent) I don't know. Just ask someone else.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are discussing the book 'Teammates'. What does the phrase 'Pee Wee's legs felt heavy' mean?

STUDENT: (Asserting his interpretation into the tumultuous crosstalk) He don't want to do it, and at the same time, he does.

TUTOR: Is that the right answer?

STUDENT: (Avoiding getting caught not knowing) I guess so.

TUTOR: Explain what you mean.

STUDENT: (Arranging to not look incompetent) I don't know. Just ask someone else.

task.content: Whole-group discussion of the book 'Teammates' by Peter Golenbock, specifically analyzing the figurative phrase 'Pee Wee’s legs felt heavy.'

context.environment: Whole-group read-aloud and reading minilesson with tumultuous crosstalk
context.social_setting: Whole-group discussion with 'turn sharks', or students ready to jump into the conversation if the speaker seemed unsure
context.time_context: During 45 min of special education reading instruction daily, usually during independent reading time

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When asserting an interpretation of a figurative phrase into tumultuous crosstalk
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Asserted an initial interpretation ('He don’t want to do it, and at the same time, he does.') into the tumultuous crosstalk
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Avoiding getting caught not knowing and arranging to not look incompetent
related_quotes: He don’t want to do it, and at the same time, he does.

behavior_patterns.condition: During standard teacher-centric IRE (Initiation-Response-Evaluation) patterns where the teacher evaluates answers based on 'rightness'
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Spends time avoiding getting caught not knowing and arranging to not look incompetent
